places.name: Branimirova podvožnjak (underpass murals)
places.description: A busy pedestrian underpass beneath Branimirova near the main station, lined with large-scale murals and rotating street-art pieces.
places.local_story: Commuters cut through here every day, so the walls get refreshed often and you see new pieces between visits. It is one of the first spots people mention when you ask where to see real paint in Zagreb, not just a museum wall.
places.practical_tip: Walk slowly in both directions; light is better mid-morning. Check the pillars and ceiling edges—artists use the whole volume, not just flat panels.
highlights: Easy link to tram and train hubs

places.name: Museum of Contemporary Art (MSU) — street art zone
places.description: Croatia's flagship contemporary museum, surrounded by open space and exterior walls where mural projects and festival pieces often appear.
places.local_story: Novi Zagreb feels quieter than the center, but MSU pulls people who care about art, not just selfies. Locals pair a ticketed exhibition with a lap around the building to see what landed on the facade or nearby walls after festivals.
places.practical_tip: Circle the museum on foot—the outdoor walls and approaches are always viewable from public space; exhibitions inside MSU follow separate hours and tickets.

places.name: Medika — Pierottijeva street art cluster
places.description: The alternative cultural center Medika and its adjoining walls, fences, and side streets dense with graffiti, stencils, and workshop energy.
places.local_story: Medika grew out of squat and DIY culture, so the paint outside matches the programming inside—concerts, workshops, and late nights. People who want the 'scene' rather than a single mural block tend to drift here.
places.practical_tip: Respect private yards and event barriers; explore the perimeter and adjacent alleys in daylight first. Evenings can be lively—check what is on before you go.
highlights: Culture hub next to the paint

places.name: Martićeva street — mural corridor
places.description: A central east–west street where building gables and long facades carry some of Zagreb's most photographed large murals.
places.local_story: Cafés and small shops line Martićeva, so you get coffee breaks between walls. Residents are used to people stopping mid-sidewalk to frame a whole gable—just step aside for bikes and trams.
places.practical_tip: Walk the full stretch between cross streets; murals sit high—bring a wide lens or simply enjoy from across the road for full impact.

places.name: Branimirova — railway & industrial wall art
places.description: Along the Branimirova / Heinzelova corridor, long walls beside tracks and service buildings hold raw graffiti pieces and occasional sanctioned murals.
places.local_story: This is the grittier side of the same artery as the underpass—freight rhythms, bridges, and concrete. Writers know these walls; casual walkers discover a different palette than the polished center.
places.practical_tip: Stay on public sidewalks and legal crossings; do not enter trackbeds. Best in daylight; listen for trams and keep awareness of traffic on wide roads.

places.name: Park Ribnjak — wall art near the Upper Town
places.description: A green pocket below the Upper Town where paths and perimeter walls feature murals and stencils in a relaxed, leafy setting.
places.local_story: Students and neighbors use Ribnjak as a breather from classes and tourists above. The mix of trees and paint feels distinctly Zagreb—history overhead, contemporary color at ground level.
places.practical_tip: Enter from multiple sides and loop the park; mural spots sit on outer walls and near playgrounds—respect families and keep dogs leashed where signed.

places.name: Tkalčićeva — back alleys & side lanes
places.description: Behind and between the famous Tkalčićeva bar strip, narrow lanes and courtyard gates hide stencils, stickers, and quick pieces away from the main pedestrian flow.
places.local_story: Everyone walks the main Tkalčićeva for drinks; locals cutting home use the parallel alleys where paint accumulates on doorframes and shutters. It is less curated than Martićeva but more intimate.
places.practical_tip: Peel off Tkalčićeva at cross streets and explore perpendicular lanes—look up at fire-escape walls and down at doorstep stickers. Evening is atmospheric but use common sense in quiet passages.
highlights: Hidden layers off the tourist strip
